recognized_artifact: Threat modeling process (e.g., STRIDE or similar structured threat analysis methodology)
branches: Evaluate may branch back to Classify if threat scope expands upon interpretation
Decide may branch back to Interpret if significance assessment requires recontextualization
candidate_missing_primitives: Mitigate (distinct from Decide — the act of applying a countermeasure, not selecting one)
Prioritize (explicitly listed as allowed but absent from the procedural sequence despite mitigation ordering being a stated goal)
ambiguities: Prioritize is in the allowed set and semantically required by 'decide which mitigations deserve attention first', yet the procedural content collapses prioritization into Decide — unclear if intentional
No Verify or Reflect step is present; unclear whether the process is intended as one-pass or iterative
Bound is used for system boundary definition but its scope (technical boundary only vs. organizational/trust boundary) is not specified
notes: The sequence is linear and terminating as written. Prioritize appears to be a missing or absorbed primitive given the explicit goal of ordering mitigations. The absence of Verify and Reflect suggests this models the analysis phase only, not a full lifecycle.
